req.title: Message to provider (audit)
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test dimensions: (1) a standard message send with audit emission (positive/EP), (2) blocking of a message with a disallowed PHI attachment (negative/NEG+DT), and (3) a boundary case around the maximum allowed message size with a clean attachment that is exactly at the size limit (boundary/BVA). A fourth state-transition test covers the full lifecycle state: draft → sent → provider-notified, verifying each AuditEvent node. Together these satisfy ≥1 positive, ≥1 negative, ≥1 boundary requirement while providing meaningful decision-table and state-transition coverage of the audit pathway.

tests.name: test_send_plain_message_emits_audit_event
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
Provider exists in the system with a known ID
actions: POST a plain-text message payload to /messages with no attachment
Capture the HTTP response and extract the message resource ID
Query the AuditEvent endpoint filtering by entity reference to the new message ID
expected: Response body contains a stored message resource with an id field
At least one AuditEvent exists whose entity.what.reference matches the message id
trace: R-HLT-100: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_send_plain_message_emits_audit_event(auth_session, api_base_url, known_provider_id):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Communication",
        "status": "completed",
        "recipient": [{"reference": f"Practitioner/{known_provider_id}"}],
        "payload": [{"contentString": "Hello, I have a question about my prescription."}]
    }
    resp = auth_session.post(f"{api_base_url}/messages", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

    message_id = resp.json()["id"]
    assert message_id, "No id returned in message response"

    audit_resp = auth_session.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Communication/{message_id}"}
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    entries = audit_resp.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 1, "No AuditEvent found for message"

    event = entries[0]["resource"]
    assert event.get("action") == "C"
    assert event.get("outcome") == "0"

tests.name: test_message_with_phi_attachment_is_blocked_and_audit_logged
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
PHI exfiltration pattern detector is active on the server
A file containing a disallowed PHI pattern (SSN regex match) is available as test fixture
actions: Construct a message payload that includes a base64-encoded attachment containing a disallowed SSN pattern
POST the payload to /messages
Query AuditEvent for the attempted operation using the patient subject reference
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity or 403 Forbidden returned
Response body contains an error code referencing PHI exfiltration policy
Message resource is NOT persisted (GET /messages returns no matching entry)
An AuditEvent is emitted with outcome != '0' indicating failure/block
trace: R-HLT-100: blocked when disallowed attachment detected
R-HLT-100: AuditEvent emitted for every operation

tests.name: test_message_attachment_at_maximum_allowed_size_accepted
preconditions: Patient is authenticated and session token is valid
System-configured maximum attachment size in bytes is retrievable via <get_max_attachment_bytes> helper
Attachment content at exactly max size contains no PHI patterns
actions: Retrieve the configured maximum attachment byte size
Generate a clean binary payload of exactly that many bytes
Base64-encode the payload and embed it in a Communication attachment
POST the message to /messages
Verify AuditEvent is emitted with success outcome
expected: Message resource id is present in the response
AuditEvent with outcome '0' (success) is emitted referencing the new message
trace: R-HLT-100: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
BVA: attachment at exact upper boundary of allowed size

tests.name: test_message_send_state_transitions_draft_to_notified_with_audit_chain
preconditions: Provider is enrolled and notification delivery is enabled
Message workflow supports draft status before submission
actions: POST a Communication resource in status 'preparation' (draft) to /messages
PATCH the same resource to status 'in-progress' (sending)
PATCH the resource to status 'completed' (sent)
Poll GET /messages/{id} until provider-notification flag is set
Collect all AuditEvents for the message resource id
expected: Final GET shows status 'completed' and notification acknowledged
Three or more AuditEvents exist covering create, update (×2), and notification states
AuditEvent sequence reflects chronological state progression
trace: R-HLT-100: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
STC: draft → sending → sent → notified state chain

tests.name: test_message_exceeding_max_attachment_size_is_rejected
preconditions: System enforces a maximum attachment byte size
Attachment content one byte above the limit contains no PHI patterns
actions: Retrieve the configured maximum attachment byte size
Generate a clean payload of exactly max_bytes + 1
Base64-encode and embed in a Communication attachment
POST the message to /messages
Query AuditEvent for a failure record
expected: HTTP 413 Payload Too Large or 422 Unprocessable Entity returned
An AuditEvent with non-zero outcome is recorded for the rejected attempt
trace: R-HLT-100: blocked when disallowed attachment detected
R-HLT-100: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
DT: rule — oversized attachment regardless of content type => reject
BVA: one byte above upper boundary
